data.title: Amber Group CEO Reflects on Impact of FTX Collapse
data.description: Amber Group CEO Michael Wu described the 2022 FTX collapse as the most significant setback of his career. According to NS3.AI, the incident led to Amber's potential $10 billion valuation plummeting. Wu noted that before the crisis, Amber employed 1,300 people globally, but the company subsequently faced severe downsizing and quarterly financial losses.

data.title: Iranian President Expresses Readiness to End Conflict with Conditions
data.description: Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi has announced the country's willingness to conclude ongoing hostilities, contingent upon receiving certain assurances. According to Odaily, Raisi emphasized the need for guarantees to ensure a peaceful resolution. The statement reflects Iran's strategic approach to international diplomacy, seeking stability while addressing security concerns.

data.title: MagicEden Wallet to Enter Export-Only Mode and Be Removed from App Stores
data.description: MagicEden's wallet is set to transition to an export-only mode and will be removed from app stores tomorrow, according to Odaily. Users who lose access to their devices or the downloaded app version will be unable to access any wallets.

data.title: Iranian President: Willing to end the war with guarantees
data.description: Iranian President Manmohan Pezechyan stated that Iran has no intention of starting a war, but is willing to end it if it can ensure that it will not be attacked again. Pezechyan called the military actions of the United States and Israel unprecedented crimes and violations of international law, and said that Iran had engaged in goodwill negotiations before the attacks. He also stated that countries hosting US military bases failed to prevent these bases from being used to attack Iran. Pezechyan pointed out that ceasing aggression is the way forward, and that Iran is willing to end the conflict if security guarantees are provided; he urged Europe to abandon its current practices and called for professional cooperation consistent with international law. (Jinshi)

data.title: Brent crude oil plunged 6.00% intraday, currently trading at $101.40 per barrel.
data.description: According to Gate data, Brent crude oil plunged 6.00% intraday, currently trading at $101.40 per barrel. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iranian President: We are prepared to end the war, but we want guarantees.
data.description: Iranian President Peskov: We are prepared to end the war, but we hope to receive guarantees. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. stocks rallied in the short term, with the Nasdaq rising over 3%, and the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both gaining over 2%.
data.description: US stocks rallied in the short term, with the Nasdaq rising over 3%, and the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both gaining over 2%. Earlier reports indicated that the Iranian president stated, "We are prepared to end the war, but we hope to receive guarantees."

data.title: Increase in Ship Traffic Through Hormuz Strait Amid Iranian Blockade
data.description: The number of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz is gradually increasing, despite Iran's ongoing blockade of most unauthorized commercial vessels. According to Odaily, Bloomberg's compiled tracking data shows that as of Monday, the average weekly two-way transit of ships rose to seven, up from five the previous week. Although this is still far from normal traffic levels, the increase indicates that a selective fleet, primarily from non-hostile Asian countries, continues to navigate the port. By allowing friendly ships to pass while restricting Western vessels, Iran's blockade has evolved into a potent economic warfare tool.

data.title: Benchmark Sets Buy Rating for Cantor Equity Partners II Amid Securitize Merger Plans
data.description: Benchmark has initiated coverage on Cantor Equity Partners II, assigning a Buy rating in light of its planned merger with Securitize. According to NS3.AI, Benchmark analysts have set a price target of $16 for Securitize, contingent upon the company achieving $178 million in sales by the end of next year. The analysts emphasized that this target also relies on Securitize expanding its competitive advantage through strategic partnerships with blue-chip companies.

data.title: During the blockade, the number of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz with Iranian approval increased.
data.description: The number of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz and the strength of their signals are slowly increasing, although Iran currently maintains an effective blockade against most unlicensed commercial vessels. According to tracking data compiled by Bloomberg, the weekly average number of ships passing through in both directions rose to seven as of Monday, up from five the previous week. While still far from normal traffic levels, this increase indicates that a small, selective fleet—primarily from non-hostile Asian countries—is continuing to transit the port. By allowing friendly vessels to pass while restricting Western vessels, Iran's blockade has evolved into a powerful tool of economic warfare. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iran and Turkey Discuss Regional Developments Amid Tensions
data.description:
Iranian Foreign Minister Aragchi and Turkish Foreign Minister Fidan held a phone conversation to discuss the latest regional developments. According to RTHK, Aragchi emphasized the responsibility of governments to condemn acts of aggression, particularly those targeting schools, research centers, historical and cultural sites, production facilities, energy infrastructure, and residential areas. Aragchi criticized U.S. officials for publicly threatening to attack Iran's energy and production facilities, labeling such statements as criminal threats that disregard international law and human rights principles. He called for a firm response from the international community.

During the conversation, Fidan referenced the recent quadrilateral foreign ministers' meeting in Islamabad, Pakistan, highlighting the regional countries' support for ending war and achieving peace and stability.

data.title: Crypto Market Structure Bill Faces Uncertain Future in Senate
data.description: TD Cowen Managing Director Jaret Seiberg has assessed the likelihood of the Clarity Act, a crypto market structure bill, advancing in the Senate and passing the House this year at one-third. According to NS3.AI, Seiberg noted that the bill is currently stalled in the Senate. He also mentioned that a recent stablecoin yield compromise proposed by Senators Thom Tillis and Angela Alsobrooks is not enough to resolve the deadlock.

data.title: Charles Hoskinson Warns of Potential Long-Term Implications of U.S. Digital Asset Market CLARITY Act
data.description: Charles Hoskinson has expressed concerns regarding the proposed U.S. Digital Asset Market CLARITY Act, suggesting it could lead to up to 15 years of regulatory rulemaking. According to NS3.AI, Hoskinson highlighted the possibility that future lawmakers might exploit the framework for their own agendas. He noted that the bill appears to favor established cryptocurrencies like Cardano, XRP, and Ethereum. Meanwhile, Congress continues to grapple with disagreements over decentralized finance and other related provisions.

data.title: Dogecoin Active Addresses Increase Despite Price Decline
data.description: Dogecoin's active addresses surged by 28% this week, reaching approximately 73,000. According to NS3.AI, this increase in activity occurred even as Dogecoin's price fell by 2.55% during the same period. The cryptocurrency remains in a bearish zone, with the four-hour chart indicating a descending triangle pattern characterized by lower lows.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC on Solana
data.description: On April 1, Whale Alert reported that the USDC Treasury has minted 250 million USDC on the Solana blockchain. According to BlockBeats, this significant minting event highlights ongoing developments within the cryptocurrency ecosystem.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Executive Highlights Treasuries Over Equities Amid Market Volatility
data.description: Goldman Sachs Asset Management executive Lindsay Rosner has expressed a preference for Treasuries over equities, citing higher yields and geopolitical uncertainty as key factors influencing market dynamics. According to NS3.AI, Rosner attributed recent market volatility to an energy shock related to the ongoing Iran conflict. She noted that investors are adjusting their expectations, anticipating fewer Federal Reserve rate cuts or even potential hikes. This shift in sentiment reflects the broader impact of geopolitical tensions on financial markets, as investors seek safer assets amid uncertainty.

data.title: TD Cowen: Crypto Market Structure Bill Has Only a One-Third Chance of Passing This Year
data.description: In a research report on Monday, TD Cowen Managing Director Jaret Seiberg stated that he is "increasingly pessimistic" about the prospects of the Clarity Act, a crypto market structure bill, believing that the probability of the bill advancing in the Senate and passing the House is only one in three. The bill is currently stalled in the US Senate, with Congress entering its two-week Easter recess. Seiberg pointed out that the recent compromise on stablecoin yields pushed by Senators Thom Tillis and Angela Alsobrooks is "insufficient" to advance the bill. This compromise prohibits offering yields on idle stablecoin balances but allows for activity-based rewards when using stablecoins. Seiberg believes this compromise may not satisfy either side: for crypto platforms, it will discourage investors from using stablecoins for liquidity management; for banks, it will incentivize crypto platforms to promote stablecoins for daily payments, threatening core deposit businesses. Seiberg stated that the bill is most likely to advance before Congress's August recess in late July, at which point the pressure of the recess may force senators to reach a compromise. He also pointed out that even senators who were previously optimistic are lowering their expectations, with Senator Mark Warner reducing the probability of the bill's passage from 80% to 50% to 60%.

data.title: The USDC Treasury minted an additional 250 million USDC on the Solana blockchain.
data.description: According to Whale Alert, the USDC Treasury minted an additional 250 million USDC on the Solana chain.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Jack Dorsey and Roelof Botha Advocate AI Over Hierarchical Structures
data.description:
On March 31, Twitter founder Jack Dorsey and Roelof Botha published an article titled "From Hierarchy to Intelligence," discussing the potential for AI to replace traditional hierarchical coordination in organizations. According to BlockBeats, the authors argue that AI will become a common model for future organizations, ending a reliance on hierarchical structures that has persisted for over two millennia.

Historically, organizational design has been based on hierarchy, a structure originating from military and industrial eras, limited by human "span of control," which typically manages 3-8 people effectively. Hierarchy functions as an "information routing protocol," but as levels increase, it significantly slows information flow and decision-making. Block is exploring the complete replacement of human hierarchical coordination with AI, transforming companies from "hierarchical organizations" to "companies built as intelligent entities." Value will shift from hierarchy to intelligence, with human roles moving to the "edges," focusing on reality, intuition, and ethics, while AI handles coordination, alignment, and proactive decision-making.

The article proposes using AI to construct two key world models to replace middle management:

Company World Model: This model is based on decision records, code, documents, and other outputs from remote work, allowing AI to continuously understand the internal operational state of the company.

Customer World Model: This model is derived from real transaction data from platforms like Cash App and Square, which Block considers money to be the most honest signal, forming a deep understanding of customers' real economic conditions.

data.title: Quantum threat escalates: Google research claims Bitcoin could be cracked in 9 minutes.
data.description: A recent study by Google's quantum AI team shows that by optimizing Shor's Algorithm, Bitcoin private keys can be derived within 9 minutes using approximately 500,000 physical qubits, a time close to the block generation cycle, presenting a realistic attack window. The study also points out that approximately 6.9 million BTC are potentially at risk due to exposed public keys, indicating that quantum attacks are moving from theory to engineering. Meanwhile, ChainXGlobal's ByteVault post-quantum hardware wallet has been officially released and is available for global sale, and is integrated into the ChainX Vault application, providing a quantum-secure asset management solution.
